publicFooter.defaultSummary: I build business systems that feel calm, fast, and useful.
publicFooter.copyright: Crafted for recruiters, hiring managers, and product teams.

homePage.identityErrorTitle: Portfolio identity could not be loaded.
homePage.identityErrorDescription: Please make sure the API is running and the public portfolio endpoints are available.
homePage.detailsWarningTitle: Identity loaded. Extra homepage sections are temporarily unavailable.
homePage.detailsWarningDescription: The hero is still using the live identity endpoint while the rest of the homepage waits for the content endpoint.
homePage.strategyCardTitle: Designed for recruiters, technical leads, and clients who want proof, not buzzwords.
homePage.strategyCardSummary: The homepage leads with real backend content, clear navigation, and focused project signals instead of decorative filler.
homePage.spotlightFallback: Homepage details are loading, but the positioning and CTA flow are already live.
homePage.techTitle: Modern frontend, reliable backend, and product-shaped architecture
homePage.techDescription: Each card reflects a real delivery area used in the existing API contracts and project content.
homePage.featuredTitle: Case studies built around systems, modules, and business outcomes
homePage.featuredDescription: These highlights make the .NET + Angular specialization obvious while staying readable for hiring and freelance conversations.
homePage.erpTitle: Enterprise workflow thinking backed by implementation detail
homePage.erpDescription: The ERP section ties together backend rules, Angular screens, reporting needs, and the case-study proof already exposed by the API.
homePage.businessTitle: Why this portfolio is positioned around outcomes, not just tooling
homePage.businessDescription: The value section turns technical strengths into the language recruiters and clients actually evaluate.

experiencePage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the public experience endpoint is reachable.
experiencePage.highlightsTitle: Short proof points instead of a long biography
experiencePage.highlightsDescription: The page is designed to stay scannable for recruiters while still showing how analytical thinking, structured training, and ERP delivery connect.
experiencePage.timelineTitle: A concise path from foundations to ERP delivery
experiencePage.timelineDescription: Each stage keeps the story short, practical, and focused on transferable value rather than long narrative text.
experiencePage.fallbackHeadline: Full-stack delivery shaped by business systems thinking
experiencePage.fallbackSummary: This page explains the journey behind the portfolio: analytical foundations, practical .NET training, and ERP-oriented implementation work.

skillsPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the public skills endpoint is reachable.
skillsPage.title: Structured strengths across the full stack
skillsPage.summary: This page keeps the stack easy to scan for recruiters and clients: primary delivery skills stand out first, while supporting capabilities stay visible without crowding the layout.
skillsPage.featuredTitle: Core tools and patterns used in real portfolio delivery
skillsPage.featuredDescription: These highlighted skills come directly from the live backend seed and make the .NET, Angular, SQL Server, and architecture strengths immediately obvious.
skillsPage.matrixTitle: Grouped by delivery area, not presented as a crowded tag wall
skillsPage.matrixDescription: Each card keeps primary skills visually stronger than the supporting layer while preserving a calm, responsive reading rhythm on mobile, tablet, laptop, and standard desktop screens.

projectsPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the public projects endpoint is reachable.
projectsPage.warningTitle: Projects could not be refreshed right now.
projectsPage.warningDescription: The latest visible results are still shown while the next request is unavailable.
projectsPage.title: Business-shaped project stories with clear technical proof
projectsPage.summary: This page presents live portfolio projects as business case studies, with concise summaries, value-focused framing, and filter controls built for recruiters, clients, and technical reviewers.
projectsPage.signalTheme: Dark, light, LTR, and RTL ready
projectsPage.emptyFeaturedDescription: As soon as the live project list is available, the strongest featured case study appears here automatically.
projectsPage.filtersTitle: Find the right project angle quickly
projectsPage.filtersDescription: Filter by project type or technology to move from broad portfolio review to specific implementation examples without losing the business context.
projectsPage.noActiveFilters: No active filters yet. Start with project type or technology to narrow the list.
projectsPage.emptyDescription: Try clearing the filters to return to the full portfolio list.

projectDetailsPage.errorTitle: This case study is temporarily unavailable.
projectDetailsPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the project details endpoint is reachable.
projectDetailsPage.notFoundDescription: The requested project slug does not map to an active case study right now.
projectDetailsPage.heroSignalTheme: Dark, light, LTR, and RTL ready
projectDetailsPage.storySignalScope: Database scope shaped for real portfolio content
projectDetailsPage.storySignalCodeFirst: EF Core Code First with ABP conventions
projectDetailsPage.storySignalReady: Existing endpoints stay ready for Angular 21
projectDetailsPage.highlightsTitle: Proof points recruiters and technical reviewers can scan quickly
projectDetailsPage.highlightsDescription: These cards summarize the strongest implementation angles without making the page feel heavy.
projectDetailsPage.databaseSpotlightTitle: A clean entity model that supports public content, contact flows, and future admin work
projectDetailsPage.databaseSpotlightDescription: This story emphasizes how the backend owns schema quality, DTO boundaries, and seed-ready SQL Server persistence while the frontend keeps a modern presentation layer.
projectDetailsPage.apiSpotlightTitle: Public endpoints that keep the frontend live while the backend keeps ownership
projectDetailsPage.apiSpotlightDescription: This story turns the portfolio site into a real API consumer: Angular stays focused on polished UX while the backend owns anonymous access rules, DTO shape, validation, and contact persistence.
projectDetailsPage.erpSpotlightTitle: A stronger read on architecture, permissions, and workflow design
projectDetailsPage.erpSpotlightDescription: This ERP case study gets extra structure because it is the clearest proof of full-stack delivery across Angular screens, backend rules, reporting, and secure business workflows.
projectDetailsPage.workflowTitle: Workflow-aware modules and protected business actions
projectDetailsPage.apiEndpointsTitle: Public endpoints that power the portfolio experience
projectDetailsPage.architectureTitle: Backend-first architecture that keeps the UI presentation-focused
projectDetailsPage.databaseStandardsTitle: Shared fields, persistence rules, and backend ownership
projectDetailsPage.apiRulesTitle: Public API rules that stay owned by the backend
projectDetailsPage.galleryTitle: Visual placeholders stay supported until project screenshots are added
projectDetailsPage.galleryDescription: The page renders real gallery items from the backend whether they are screenshots or placeholder slots for future visuals.
projectDetailsPage.databaseAcceptanceTitle: What this story needed to satisfy before it was done
projectDetailsPage.apiAcceptanceTitle: What Story 4.3 needed to satisfy before it was done
projectDetailsPage.linksTitle: Use live links when they exist
projectDetailsPage.linksDescription: GitHub and live-demo actions only appear when the backend exposes them for the current project.
projectDetailsPage.apiLiveTitle: Real API surfaces the page can verify right now
projectDetailsPage.apiLiveDescription: These cards are built from the existing public endpoints already wired into the app, so the design showcases real data instead of mock payloads.
projectDetailsPage.apiLiveErrorTitle: Live endpoint preview is temporarily unavailable.
projectDetailsPage.apiLiveErrorDescription: The case study still renders, but the preview cards need the backend API to be reachable.
projectDetailsPage.apiProjectsCardSummary: The list endpoint provides live recruiter-facing cards plus project-type filter metadata for the projects page.
projectDetailsPage.apiProjectDetailsCardSummary: This case-study page itself is loaded from the slug endpoint, proving the public details contract in the browser.
projectDetailsPage.apiSkillsCardSummary: The skills endpoint returns categorized groups so the public skills page stays readable and backend-driven.
projectDetailsPage.apiExperienceCardSummary: The experience endpoint keeps the about page backed by timeline items and highlight badges from the backend.
projectDetailsPage.apiSettingsCardSummary: The settings endpoint exposes presentation-supporting values without hardcoding them into Angular.
projectDetailsPage.apiContactCardSummary: The contact endpoint accepts real form submissions, validates them on the backend, and saves them for later review.
projectDetailsPage.apiContactCardCaption: required fields, email validation, and spam honeypot

contactPage.title: Start a product conversation through the real backend flow
contactPage.summary: This page stays intentionally simple and modern while proving Story 4.3 in practice: public settings load from the API, and the contact form posts to the real backend instead of a placeholder service.
contactPage.signalTheme: Responsive, dark/light, LTR, and RTL ready
contactPage.statusTitle: Two public endpoints power this page directly
contactPage.statusDescription: The contact page is both a recruiter-friendly surface and a small proof that the public API layer is already usable from Angular 21.
contactPage.siteSettingsEndpointSummary: Loads branded public settings for presentation support.
contactPage.contactEndpointSummary: Submits validated contact messages to the backend database.
contactPage.formTitle: Share the role, product, or workflow you need help with
contactPage.formDescription: The frontend keeps the experience clear and responsive while the backend remains the source of truth for validation and message persistence.
contactPage.messagePlaceholder: Tell me about the role, project scope, or business problem you want to solve.
contactPage.backendNote: Submission is validated and saved by the backend API, not simulated in the browser.
contactPage.errorDescription: The request could not be completed. Please make sure the backend API is running and try again.
contactPage.linksTitle: Public profile routes already come from live backend content
contactPage.linksDescription: These links are loaded from the public home-page contract so the contact view stays connected to the rest of the portfolio experience.
contactPage.settingsTitle: Public settings coming from the API
contactPage.settingsDescription: These values are loaded from `/api/site-settings`, which keeps branded copy and other public presentation settings out of hardcoded Angular placeholders.
contactPage.validationMinLength: Please add a little more detail.
contactPage.validationDisposableEmail: Please use a real work or personal email.

adminSidebar.caption: Admin workspace
adminSidebar.helper: Protected operations for projects, skills, experience, and messages.

adminLoginPage.title: Sign in to manage projects, skills, experience, and messages.
adminLoginPage.description: This area stays visually separate from the public portfolio and keeps future backend integrations ready.
securityPoints: Clear separation between public presentation and admin workflows.
Responsive layout for phone, tablet, laptop, and large desktop screens.
Theme and language controls stay consistent with the public experience.

notFoundPage.title: Page not found
notFoundPage.body: The route you requested is not part of the current portfolio workspace.
